packs.title: Creator Pack: Developer Fire Drill
packs.description: A creator-made incident response side pack about production bugs, rollback, postmortem discipline, and stakeholder communication.

chapters.title: Creator Pack: Production Fire Drill
chapters.story: A creator-made side pack drops you into a release room where the login graph looks like a falling cricket score. The client wants a fix now, the mentor wants evidence, and the team wants lunch.
chapters.task: Handle the incident without creating a second incident.
chapters.learningObjective: Practice incident triage and controlled rollback communication.
safetyGuardrails: Do not patch without rollback and verification.
chapters.safeExplanation: Incident response needs evidence, rollback readiness, and stakeholder updates.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Stabilize first, explain clearly, then fix with proof.
chapters.professionalLearningPoint: The fastest fix is not professional if it removes traceability.

choices.text: Rollback to the last known safe build, announce impact, and start evidence-based triage.

outcome.title: Controlled Fire Drill
outcome.description: The room is still tense, but nobody is inventing mystery fixes at 2x speed. The client gets a clear update and the team gets a reproducible path.
outcome.moralLesson: In incidents, calm evidence beats heroic guessing.
outcome.consequenceSummary: You reduced incident risk and improved team confidence.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Good. You acted like a release owner, not a keyboard magician.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use rollback, monitoring, and communication before attempting risky changes.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Always pair incident action with stakeholder communication.

choices.text: Push a hotfix directly because the error looks obvious and confidence is free.

outcome.title: Confidence.exe Has Crashed
outcome.description: The hotfix fixes login and breaks logout. The client asks if this is a feature. Your mentor develops a headache with sprint velocity.
outcome.moralLesson: Confidence without verification is just chaos wearing sunglasses.
outcome.consequenceSummary: You created a regression risk and unlocked a cleanup mission.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Fast, yes. Safe, no. That was not a fix; that was a plot twist.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Production changes require verification and rollback readiness.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Never let pressure remove your release discipline.

choices.text: Ask the QA lead for last failing evidence before choosing between rollback and patch.

outcome.title: Evidence Before Ego
outcome.description: QA shares the reproduction path. The senior dev nods. Somewhere, a dashboard quietly stops sweating.
outcome.moralLesson: Good incident work is collaborative, not solo hero drama.
outcome.consequenceSummary: You improved team trust and kept the fix evidence-based.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: This is how adults debug: evidence first, ego later if required never.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Ask for reproduction evidence before committing to production action.

chapters.title: Creator Pack: The Postmortem Without Finger-Pointing
chapters.story: The incident is over. Now comes the meeting where everyone pretends they definitely knew the root cause yesterday.
chapters.task: Lead a blameless postmortem that creates real improvement.
chapters.learningObjective: Practice blameless review and action-item discipline.
safetyGuardrails: Do not blame individuals for system failures.
chapters.safeExplanation: Postmortems should improve systems, not punish people for surfacing issues.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: A useful postmortem turns evidence into owned improvements.
chapters.professionalLearningPoint: Blameless postmortems protect learning and accountability together.

choices.text: Map timeline, root cause, missed signals, and owners for preventive actions.

outcome.title: Postmortem With Spine
outcome.description: The team leaves with action items instead of dramatic quotes for office folklore.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Team trust improved through structured learning.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Good postmortem. Nobody cried, and the system actually improved.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Document facts, contributing conditions, and preventive actions.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Always close postmortems with owners and dates.

choices.text: Blame the intern because their Jira comment used lowercase letters.

outcome.title: Blame Culture DLC
outcome.description: Everyone learns one thing: hide mistakes faster next time. Productivity goes down and side-eye goes up.
outcome.moralLesson: Blame hides risk; accountability fixes systems.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Team trust dropped and future incident reporting may weaken.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: That was not leadership. That was a keyboard-powered scapegoat festival.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Avoid blame; focus on process and system improvement.

choices.text: Create a short public client update and a longer internal action register.

outcome.title: Two Audiences, One Truth
outcome.description: The client gets clarity without internal noise. The team gets details without performative panic.
outcome.moralLesson: Professional communication changes depth, not truth.
outcome.consequenceSummary: Client trust improved through clear communication.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Excellent. Transparency without oversharing is a professional superpower.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Communicate accurate impact, mitigation, and next steps.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Tailor communication to audience while preserving truth.

packs.title: Creator Pack: Safe Triage Practice
packs.description: Draft pack reserved for safe triage and communication practice. Not published until medical safety review is approved.
